pests.name: Leaf Hopper
pests.solution: Control leaf hopper with recommended insecticides to minimize disease spread.

diseases.name: Damping Off
diseases.causal_agent: Pythium Spp., Phytophthora Spp., Fusarium Spp., Rhizoctonia Spp., Sclerotium Rolfsii
diseases.impact: Seedlings collapse at the base of the stem. Roots rot and seedlings die. Rhizoctonia spp. causes brown-black discoloration at the base.
diseases.solution: Prepare nursery beds in uncultivated, well-drained land. Sterilize nursery beds (burning, solarization, or chemicals). Remove infected plants and surrounding soil. Seed treatment (Thiram 80% WP - 5g/kg, Thiophanate-methyl 50% WP + Thiram 30% WP - 4g/kg, Captan 50% WP - 6g/kg). Soil treatment (localized application around plant base with recommended fungicides: Thiophanate-methyl 50% WP + Thiram 30% WP - 50g/50l/10m2, Thiram 80% WP - 70g/50l/10m2, Captan 50% WP - 60g/50l/10m2).

diseases.name: Stem Rot / Foot Rot
diseases.impact: Yellowing of leaves. Decay of the basal area. Presence of fungal mycelial mats or brown mats at the base.
diseases.solution: Destroy infected plants and surrounding soil. Improve drainage. Deep plowing and exposing infected soil to strong sunlight. Prevent root damage during transplanting. Soil treatment (localized application around plant base with recommended fungicides: Thiophanate-methyl 50% WP + Thiram 30% WP - 50g/50l/10m2, Thiram 80% WP - 70g/50l/10m2, Captan 50% WP - 60g/50l/10m2).

diseases.name: Fusarium Wilt
diseases.impact: Yellowing and wilting of leaves. Discoloration of vascular tissue in the stem. Root rot and plant death.
diseases.solution: Destroy infected plants and surrounding soil. Improve drainage. Deep plowing and exposing infected soil to strong sunlight. Prevent root damage during transplanting. Soil treatment (localized application around plant base with recommended fungicides: Thiophanate-methyl 50% WP + Thiram 30% WP - 50g/50l/10m2, Thiram 80% WP - 70g/50l/10m2, Captan 50% WP - 60g/50l/10m2).

diseases.name: Powdery Mildew
diseases.impact: Round yellow spots or patches appear on the lower epidermis of leaves. White powdery residues are seen on the upper epidermis of leaves.
diseases.solution: Use recommended fungicides at early stages of disease development (Sulfur 80% WG 50g/10l water, Chlorothalonil 500g/l SC - 30ml/10l water, Carbendazim 50% WP - 7g/10l water).

diseases.name: Anthracnose
diseases.impact: Sunken circular spots with copper to orange-colored black concentric rings in the center. Pink conidia (spores) exude from the lesions.
diseases.solution: Use healthy seeds. Avoid excessive irrigation. Remove and destroy infected fruits. Seed treatment (Thiram 80% WP - 5g/kg, Thiophanate-methyl 50% WP + Thiram 30% WP - 4g/kg, Captan 50% WP - 6g/kg). Apply fungicides during flowering in rainy season (Flusilazole 500g/l SC - 10ml/10l water, Metiram 55% + Pyraclostrobin 5% WP - 20g/10l water, Carbendazim 50% WG - 7g/10l water, Thiophanate-methyl 70% WP - 6g/10l water, Chlorothalonil 500 SC - 30ml/10l water).

diseases.name: Phomopsis Blight
diseases.impact: Small gray canker spots with black margins appear on stems and veins. Centers of spots turn brown to black due to conidia development. Soft and watery fruit spots turn black and become mummified.
diseases.solution: Remove and destroy infected fruits. Use recommended fungicides at early stages of disease development (Chlorothalonil 500g/l SC - 30ml/10l water, Carbendazim 50% WP - 7g/10l water, Thiophanate-methyl 70% WP - 6g/10l water).

diseases.name: Bacterial Wilt
diseases.impact: Single branches/stems show slight wilting. Rapid and permanent wilting of the entire plant. Discoloration of vascular tissue. When infected parts are cut and immersed in clear water, a thin viscous bacterial ooze emerges from the cut end.
diseases.solution: Remove and destroy infected plants along with soil. Destroy crop residues after harvest. Crop rotation with non-susceptible crops (cruciferous vegetables and okra) helps reduce disease. Use resistant varieties. Clean and sterilize all farm equipment/tools. No chemical control methods.

diseases.name: Little Leaf Disease (Witches' Broom)
diseases.impact: Leaves are narrow, soft, smooth, and yellow. Internodes of the stem become shortened. Auxiliary buds get enlarged but their petioles and leaves remain shortened, giving the plant a bushy appearance. Flowers remain green/no flowering. Fruiting is rare.
diseases.solution: Remove and burn infected plants. Control leaf hopper (Hishimonus phycitiy) with recommended insecticides to minimize disease spread.
